title: /mes
text: /mes Compile global messages, help texts, and language setups. /mes { /aft= DateTime } { /bef= DateTime } { Library } Switches /aft= or /bef= Parameters Library —name of the library containing the objects to compile; if omitted, global messages, help texts, and language setups are compiled. Use wildcard characters to specify multiple libraries. Use With ide.exe —Uniface IDE Description The location of compiled messages is determined by the $RSO path. Related Links Compiling from the IDE Command Line The IDE command line can be used to compile multiple objects without opening the IDE. It is especially useful for automating compilation by means of batch scripts. Messages Messages are language-related objects that enable you to deploy a Uniface application in different languages and locales. They are stored in language-specific libraries, and Uniface automatically chooses the appropriate library at runtime based on the configuration. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
